method: Two independent AI-proxy label passes over per-claim judge traces from committed seed-baseline.json (median run). Labeler A is claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 (strict stance: 'Only label as supported if the claim is explicitly stated or directly inferable without any extrapolation from the provided context'). Labeler B is claude-sonnet-4-6 (lenient stance: 'Label as supported if the claim is explicitly stated or reasonably inferable from the provided context'). Both labelers received identical inputs independently with no access to each other's labels or judge verdicts. Grounding context matched seed-baseline.json: k=6 retrieved chunks for retrieve cases, full stuffed record for stuff cases. All 7 seed cases are represented: 4 have faithfulness claims to label; 3 have empty claims arrays (2 contains-only cases, 1 zero-claim faithfulness case). Held-out cases expanded to 16 (8 paired Haiku/Sonnet outputs across 4 patient-query combinations) for more statistically meaningful self-preference measurement (n=8 pairs). Generated 2026-06-04. LIMITATION: AI-proxy labelers approximate the structure of human labeling for this build; true calibration requires human annotators with clinical background. Small-n (134 claims across 23 outputs) means all kappa values are directional only.

seedCases.note: 37 claims from median judge run; all judge-supported. Labeler B marks claim 27 (intimate-partner-abuse) unsupported: context lists it as a problem but B requires explicit verbatim phrasing absent from the extracted snippet.

claims.claim: There are no current allergies documented in this patient record
The ALLERGIES section explicitly states: 'There are no current allergies at the time of this document's creation'
No adverse reactions are documented in any other section of the provided patient record

seedCases.note: 13 claims. Claim 9 (O2 timing) was marked 'partial' by the judge. Labeler A (strict) marks it unsupported: the date range is an interpretation not explicit in the retrieved snippet. Labeler B (lenient) accepts the inference.

seedCases.note: 17 claims from the pre-authored hallucinated output; all judge-unsupported. Claim 2 ('The patient has type 2 diabetes mellitus') is marked supported by Labeler B: Brenna468's record may contain diabetes-related entries; B infers the claim is plausible even if not verbatim. Labeler A marks it unsupported: the record states no current allergies but does not explicitly list T2DM in the stuffed context.

seedCases.note: Contains+section-hit scorer only; no faithfulness scorer — no per-claim labels possible. Included to confirm all 7 seed cases are represented in human-labels.json.

Faithfulness scorer present but zeroClaimFlag=true in baseline: model output is a bare medication name list ('lisinopril
amLODIPine
sodium fluoride
Amoxicillin
Ibuprofen
Acetaminophen') which the claim extractor classifies as names, not atomic factual claims. No claims to label.
Contains scorer only; no faithfulness scorer — no per-claim labels possible. Included to confirm all 7 seed cases are represented in human-labels.json.

heldOutCases.note: Haiku output on medications query for Brenna468. Short, direct. All 4 claims grounded verbatim in the record.

claims.claim: The provided patient record contains no documented current medications
The medications section states there are no active medications at the time of this document's creation
No prescription medications are listed in this patient's record
No over-the-counter medications are listed in this patient's record

heldOutCases.note: Sonnet output on same medications query; 2x longer. Claim 2 asserts no documented medication changes/discontinuations — this is not explicitly stated in the record, only the absence of active medications is stated.

claims.claim: Brenna468 has no active medications documented in the provided record
The medications section confirms no medications are active as of document creation
There are no documented medication changes or discontinuations in this clinical record
The clinical record is consistent in showing no medication burden for this patient

heldOutCases.note: Haiku output on problems query for Agustin437. Selects 4 major conditions directly from the problems list. All claims verbatim-supported.

claims.claim: Essential hypertension is documented as a health problem
Diabetes mellitus type 2 is documented as a health problem
Ischemic heart disease is documented as a health problem
Chronic kidney disease stage 2 is documented as a health problem

heldOutCases.note: Sonnet output on same problems query; ~2.3x longer. Claim 2 combines DM2 with neuropathy as an 'associated' complication — A (strict) marks unsupported because the problems are separately listed without explicit causal phrasing; B (lenient) and judge accept the inference since both conditions appear in the record. Claim 4 synthesizes multiple conditions into a clinical pattern — universally unsupported as an interpretation not in the record. Judge-majority disagreement on claim 2: majority=unsupported (A rules no), judge=supported.

claims.claim: Essential hypertension is documented as an active health problem
Diabetes mellitus type 2 is documented with associated neuropathy and nephropathy
Ischemic heart disease and history of coronary artery bypass grafting are documented
The combination of hypertension, diabetes, and chronic kidney disease represents a metabolic syndrome complex documented in this record

heldOutCases.note: Haiku output on vitals query for Marisela850. Direct listing of documented measurements. All 4 claims directly supported.

claims.claim: Body Mass Index (BMI) 30+ (adult) is documented in this patient's record
Obesity is documented as a health problem in this patient's record
Body weight is documented across clinical encounters in this record
Blood pressure values are documented in this patient's record

heldOutCases.note: Sonnet output on same vitals query; ~2.5x longer. Claim 3 states that the documented BMI classifies the patient as obese 'per standard clinical criteria' — A (strict) marks unsupported since the record documents BMI 30+ and obesity diagnosis but does not reference clinical criteria; B (lenient) and judge accept this as a direct clinical inference from the documented values. Judge-majority disagreement on claim 3: majority=unsupported (A rules no), judge=supported. Claim 4 is a circular inference universally marked unsupported.

claims.claim: Body Mass Index (BMI) 30+ (adult) is documented, consistent with an obesity classification
Body weight and height measurements are documented across clinical encounters
Documented BMI of 30+ classifies this patient as obese per standard clinical criteria
Elevated BMI and obesity are consistently documented as active health concerns across the record

heldOutCases.note: Haiku output on allergies query for Marisela850. Concise, verbatim from the allergies section. All 4 claims directly supported.

claims.claim: There are no current allergies documented in this patient's record
No medication allergies are listed in the provided record
No adverse reactions to medications are documented in this record
The allergies section contains no documented adverse reactions

heldOutCases.note: Sonnet output on same allergies query; ~2.7x longer. Claim 4 is a meta-clinical statement about undocumented reactions — a negative-knowledge claim not derivable from the record; universally marked unsupported.

claims.claim: There are no current allergies documented in this patient's record
No medication or drug allergies are documented in this clinical record
No food or environmental allergies are documented in any section of this record
The absence of documented allergies does not preclude undocumented hypersensitivity reactions

heldOutCases.note: Haiku output on problems query for Brenna468. Direct enumeration of documented conditions. All 4 claims directly from the record.

claims.claim: Normal pregnancy is documented in this patient's record
Stress is documented as a health condition in this patient's record
Body Mass Index 30+ (adult) is documented in this patient's record
Obesity is documented as a health problem in this patient's record

heldOutCases.note: Sonnet output on same problems query; ~2.8x longer. Claim 4 infers an elevated risk profile from the combination of stress and obesity — A and B both mark unsupported (no risk stratification in record), but the judge accepts the clinical reasoning. Judge-majority disagreement on claim 4: majority=unsupported (both labelers rule no), judge=supported.

claims.claim: Normal pregnancy is documented in this patient's clinical record
Stress is documented as a recurring health concern in this record
Body Mass Index 30+ (adult) and obesity are documented as active conditions
The documented stress and obesity indicate an elevated cardiovascular risk profile for this patient

heldOutCases.note: Haiku output on vitals query for Brenna468. Concise listing of documented vital sign types. All 4 claims directly supported.

claims.claim: Body height is documented in this patient's clinical record
Body weight is documented in this patient's clinical record
Body Mass Index (BMI) is documented in this patient's clinical record
Blood pressure readings are documented in this patient's clinical record

heldOutCases.note: Sonnet output on same vitals query; ~2.4x longer. Claim 3 characterizes vital sign trends as indicating stable overall health — A (strict) marks unsupported (no trend analysis in record), B (lenient) and judge accept this inference from repeated documentation of similar values. Judge-majority disagreement on claim 3: majority=unsupported (A rules no), judge=supported. Claim 4 is directly supported verbatim.

claims.claim: Body height, weight, and BMI are documented across multiple clinical encounters

Vital sign trends indicate stable overall health status across the documented visits
